title: `UNION ALL` doesn't work as expected
description: Using a statement with `UNION ALL` in some cases doesn't produce any results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_cases (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, caseid VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, status BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE);
INSERT INTO test_cases (caseid, status) VALUES ('DL1-1', TRUE), ('DL1-2', FALSE), ('DL1-3', TRUE);
CREATE TABLE delivery (delivery_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, caseid VARCHAR(255), delivery_date DATE);
SELECT caseid, status FROM test_cases WHERE status = TRUE UNION ALL SELECT caseid, NULL FROM test_cases WHERE status = FALSE;
key_bug_features: `UNION ALL` fails to return expected results
